one_time_description: Contribution from 1907 Surrender Trust for land acquisition
economic_notes: Fishing Lake First Nation Ventures Limited Partnership (99.99% owned) and Fishing Lake First Nation Ventures Ltd (100% owned) are government business enterprises accounted for under modified equity method. Net loss from GBEs was $77,201 in 2022. New school construction project underway with $2,265,075 in restricted cash. Treaty 4 Benefits Claim being pursued with RBC term loan of $570,268.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2022 (not 2024). Financial statements signed December 20, 2022. One subsidiary fully consolidated (Fishing Lake Indian Child & Family Services Inc.) and two GBEs under modified equity (FLFN Ventures LP and FLFN Ventures Ltd). Fishing Lake 1907 Surrender Trust is excluded from consolidation as it is governed by appointed trustees. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($17,713,658) + CMHC ($418,669) + Trust funds federal ($106,185) + FSIN ($2,918) + SITAG ($183,952) = $20,425,382 approximately; Surrender Trust and BHP may also be considered government-related. Large deferred revenue balance of $7,778,981 in ISC unexpended funds. Net debt position of $2,442,697. Long-term debt is a new RBC term loan of $570,268 for Treaty 4 Benefits Claim. CMHC mortgage note was truncated in source document.
